description: V1-4 falsifiability-metric baselines (osojicode/work#27). V1-7's corpus evaluator builds on these; a climbing escalation rate or CE-gap rate signals the Claim Builder schema / taxonomy needs revision.

bootstrap_set.ce_gap_note: 23/54 findings carry gap_type=uncategorized — dominated by the V1-2 adapters defaulting obligation_* (contract) and doc_* (description) findings. The metric is correctly flagging adapter under-classification; V1-5 native Finding emission resolves it.
bootstrap_set.gate: non-gray < 5% (spec 0001 verification criterion 5): MET at r4

rubric-level judgment: claim mode confirms a 2-line delegating wrapper as dead (519 files swept, 282 class refs, zero method calls); adjudication dismissed on Significance. Owned by V1-5 rubric work.

live_debris_corpus.note: The V1-3 inline builder left 34/62 eligible debris findings unverified (no FactsDB evidence); the mechanized builders fill all 62 (evidence-of-absence with honest scope). Production Phase 3 triages ~2.2x more debris claims, each with a real verdict (V1-5e later retired the legacy debris prompt for the unified rubric; ab-v15e-report.md).
